summary: Re-tested AI Advisor backend after multi-turn context fix. All 7/7 pytest cases pass (15.74s), including the previously failing test_multi_turn_context where Claude correctly recalls '4271' across two requests within the same session_id. Empty-message HTTP 400 and history persistence/chronology paths still work. Fix in /app/backend/server.py loads up to 20 prior messages from db.chat_messages and injects them as a transcript prefix into the user message before calling Claude.

minor.issue: History cap is 20 messages (not 20 turns). to_list(40)[-20:] keeps last 20 individual messages, so effectively ~10 user/assistant pairs. Comment in code says '20 turni' which is misleading vs actual behavior.
No upper bound on req.message length — a 1MB message would still be accepted; consider Pydantic max_length on ChatRequest.message.
503 path (missing EMERGENT_LLM_KEY) is not covered by automated tests because env var is set; verified by code inspection only.

action_items: Optional: align comment vs behavior — either bump cap to 40 messages (=20 turns) or update the comment to '20 messaggi (~10 turni)'.
Optional: add max_length=4000 on ChatRequest.message to prevent abusive payloads.
Optional: add a unit test that monkey-patches EMERGENT_LLM_KEY to '' to assert HTTP 503 path.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:553-556 — fix is clean: loads prior history before persisting the new user message (avoids echoing the same turn twice), then caps to last 20 messages.

server.py:567-579 — transcript is injected as plain text in the user message rather than via LlmChat.add_message API. Works because Claude is good at parsing the [CRONOLOGIA] / [NUOVO MESSAGGIO] block, but adds tokens vs proper role-separated messages. Acceptable trade-off given emergentintegrations LlmChat does not auto-rehydrate.
server.py:589-591 — exception is now logged with logger.exception and the client gets a generic message — improvement over iteration 2 which leaked raw error.
server.py:556 — the cap '20' is a magic number; would benefit from a module-level constant MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES.

Token-cost note: every request now re-sends the entire transcript (up to 20 msgs) AND the financial context. For long sessions this scales linearly with turns; the localStorage session_id (frontend) is never rotated, so cost will grow indefinitely until DELETE history or a 'New chat' button is added.

success_rate.frontend: not retested (no FE changes per request)

seed_data_creation: Used seeded financials (fatturato 480000, EBITDA 206200). Test sessions created with unique 'test-ai-*' session_ids and cleaned via DELETE /api/ai-advisor/history.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI Advisor backend is fully green on iteration 3. Multi-turn context fix verified end-to-end with real Claude calls. Only minor code-quality items remain (history cap comment, max_length validation, 503-path test). Frontend on /ai-advisor was already 100% in iteration 2 and was not re-tested per main agent instruction.

rca of the issue.bug: RESOLVED — Multi-turn AI conversation now retains context across requests within the same session_id.
rca of the issue.fix_summary: server.py ai_advisor_chat now (1) reads prior messages for session_id from MongoDB BEFORE saving the new user message, (2) caps to last 20, (3) builds a transcript prefix '[CRONOLOGIA CONVERSAZIONE PRECEDENTE] ... [FINE CRONOLOGIA]' and concatenates with the new user message, (4) sends the combined text via LlmChat.send_message. Claude reliably recalls the prior turn — verified test 'Ricorda 4271' -> 'Qual era il codice?' returns reply containing '4271'.
